hero.headline: Bharati Vidyapeeth Online MBA in Hospitality: Fees, Syllabus and Career Scope
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved 2-year programme | NAAC A+ accredited | NIRF #59 University | Rs 1,60,000 total | Rare online MBA specialisation

sections.tldr: Bharati Vidyapeeth Online MBA in Hospitality is a rare online MBA specialisation. Very few NIRF-ranked universities offer Hospitality as a dedicated online MBA spec. Rs 1,60,000 flat total across 4 semesters with zero-cost EMI. NAAC A+, NIRF #59 University, UGC-DEB approved. Covers hotel operations management, tourism management, food and beverage management, and hospitality marketing. Pune's tourism circuit (Lonavala, Mahabaleshwar, Pune heritage) provides regional industry context.

about.body:
Bharati Vidyapeeth (Deemed to be University) is a Pune-based institution established in 1964. It holds NAAC A+ accreditation, NIRF #59 University ranking (2025), and UGC-DEB approval for online programmes.

The Hospitality specialisation is a rare offering in the online MBA space. Most online MBA programmes focus on Finance, Marketing, HR, and IT. Hospitality as a dedicated specialisation is uncommon, making Bharati Vidyapeeth one of the few NIRF-ranked options for hospitality professionals seeking an MBA.

Pune sits at the centre of a strong tourism circuit. Lonavala, Khandala, Mahabaleshwar, and Lavasa are weekend destinations that drive hospitality demand year-round. Pune itself hosts corporate hotels, convention centres, and a growing food and beverage scene. Maharashtra is one of India's top states for domestic and international tourism.

Bharati Vidyapeeth offers 14 MBA specialisations, the widest among Indian online universities. This breadth reflects decades of distance learning operations where the university built faculty and curriculum across niche areas including hospitality.

The Rs 1,60,000 fee is competitive for a NIRF-ranked hospitality MBA. Most hospitality management programmes at the postgraduate level cost significantly more, especially campus-based options.

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Detailed Sem 3-4 curriculum for Bharati Vidyapeeth's Hospitality specialisation is shared at admission. Our counsellor can share the current subject list within 24 hours.

whoHires.body:
Hospitality MBA graduates target management roles across hotels, tourism, food service, and travel industries:

**Industries:** Hotels and resorts, restaurant chains, airline catering, cruise lines, tourism departments, travel agencies, convention centres, theme parks, food delivery platforms, hospitality technology companies

**Entry-level roles:** Front Office Executive, F&B Supervisor, Guest Relations Officer, Revenue Associate, Travel Operations Coordinator, Hospitality Marketing Executive

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years):** Hotel Operations Manager, F&B Manager, Revenue Manager, Tourism Development Officer, Travel Business Manager, Banquet Manager, Guest Experience Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** General Manager (Hotel), VP Hotel Operations, Director of Revenue, Head of Tourism Development, Chief Operating Officer (Hospitality Chain), Regional Director

**Employers:** Taj Hotels, ITC Hotels, Oberoi Hotels, Marriott India, Hyatt India, Lemon Tree Hotels, OYO Rooms, MakeMyTrip, Thomas Cook India, IRCTC, Maharashtra Tourism Development Corporation, and Pune's growing hotel and restaurant industry.

skills.body:
The Hospitality specialisation builds management skills across hotel operations, tourism, and food service:

**Technical skills:**
- Hotel Operations: front office management, housekeeping management, property management systems, and guest lifecycle management from booking to checkout
- Revenue Management: dynamic pricing strategy, occupancy optimization, channel management (OTA vs direct bookings), and RevPAR analysis
- Food and Beverage Management: menu engineering, food cost control, kitchen operations management, banquet planning, and food safety compliance (FSSAI)
- Tourism Management: destination marketing, tour package design, cultural tourism development, and sustainable tourism planning

**Applied skills:**
- Hospitality Marketing: online reputation management (TripAdvisor, Google Reviews), social media marketing for hotels, loyalty programme design, and corporate tie-up strategy
- Hospitality Finance: hotel P&L management, capex planning for property upgrades, and franchise vs management contract evaluation

**Soft skills:** Guest complaint resolution, cross-cultural communication with international guests, team management across shifts, and stakeholder management with property owners and franchisors.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Bharati Vidyapeeth Online MBA Hospitality against alternatives:

**vs General Management MBAs:** Most students interested in hospitality management end up choosing General Management or Marketing MBAs because dedicated Hospitality specs are rare online. A General Management MBA provides broad business skills but lacks hospitality-specific subjects like revenue management, F&B operations, and hotel operations. If hospitality is your career, a dedicated spec is more targeted.

**vs Amity Online MBA Hospitality Management (Rs 2,25,000):** Amity offers Hospitality Management at Rs 2,25,000 with NAAC A+ and NIRF #22. Amity wins on brand recognition and higher NIRF rank. Bharati Vidyapeeth is Rs 65,000 cheaper with zero-cost EMI. For hospitality professionals on tight budgets, Bharati Vidyapeeth offers NIRF credentials at a significantly lower cost.

**vs Campus-based Hotel Management PG Programmes:** IHM and campus-based hotel management institutes offer full-time PG programmes with hands-on training. For working hospitality professionals who cannot leave their jobs, an online MBA is the only option. Bharati Vidyapeeth provides that option with NIRF ranking.

**Positioning:** Hospitality as an online MBA spec is rare. Bharati Vidyapeeth is one of the few NIRF-ranked options at a competitive Rs 1,60,000 price point.

fees.body: Bharati Vidyapeeth Online MBA in Hospitality is Rs 1,60,000 flat total across 4 semesters. Zero-cost EMI available directly through the university. Contact our counsellor for registration, application fees, and current EMI tenure options. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Bharati Vidyapeeth each quarter. Please reconfirm with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an MBA degree certificate with Hospitality as the specialisation, issued by Bharati Vidyapeeth (Deemed to be University), Pune.

The degree carries NAAC A+ accreditation, NIRF #59 University ranking, and UGC-DEB approval. Online mode is identified per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. Valid for corporate hiring in hotel chains, tourism departments, government hospitality roles, and higher education admission across India.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from Bharati Vidyapeeth Online MBA Hospitality students. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Rohit
items.liked: Bharati Vidyapeeth has been a Pune institution for 60 years. The hospitality MBA is rare online. Revenue management and hotel operations subjects were directly relevant to my hotel career
items.disliked: Syllabus was shared only after admission. More pre-enrollment transparency would be helpful
items.body: I work at a Pune hotel and needed an MBA to move into operations management. Hospitality as an online MBA spec is almost impossible to find. Bharati Vidyapeeth was one of the only NIRF-ranked options. The Rs 1.6L fee is very reasonable compared to full-time hotel management PG programmes.

items.name: Anjali
items.liked: F&B management and tourism planning subjects were applicable to Goa's hospitality market. NAAC A+ ranking gives the degree credibility with hotel chains
items.disliked: Brand recognition outside Maharashtra is not as strong as Symbiosis or Manipal for hospitality
items.body: Goa's hotel and tourism industry needs hospitality management professionals. Most online MBAs do not offer hospitality as a specialisation. Bharati Vidyapeeth filled this gap at an affordable fee. Tourism management and hospitality marketing content aligned with my resort management career.

items.name: Manish
items.liked: NIRF #59 ranking adds value. Hospitality specialisation is genuinely rare in online MBA programmes
items.disliked: The LMS could be more modern. Some sessions felt pre-recorded rather than interactive
items.body: Udaipur's heritage hotel market is niche and competitive. I needed formal hospitality management credentials without leaving my hotel job. Bharati Vidyapeeth was one of the few options available online. Content was functional and covered hotel operations, F&B, and revenue management at a practical level.

faqs.question: Why does Bharati Vidyapeeth offer 14 MBA specialisations?
faqs.answer: Bharati Vidyapeeth has decades of distance learning heritage that preceded the online pivot. The university built infrastructure and faculty across 14 specialisation areas during the distance education era. This legacy gives it the widest MBA specialisation portfolio among Indian online universities.

faqs.question: Is the syllabus available before admission?
faqs.answer: The detailed Sem 3-4 subject list for Hospitality is shared at admission. Our counsellor can share the current semester-wise subject breakdown within 24 hours before you commit to enrollment.

faqs.question: Is Hospitality MBA different from Hotel Management degrees?
faqs.answer: Yes. Hotel Management degrees (BHM, MHMCT) focus on operational skills like food production, front office operations, and housekeeping. An MBA in Hospitality focuses on business management within the hospitality industry: revenue management, hospitality marketing, operations strategy, and financial management. The MBA is suited for management-level career progression.

faqs.question: Is Bharati Vidyapeeth Online MBA UGC-DEB approved?
faqs.answer: Yes. Bharati Vidyapeeth (Deemed to be University) holds UGC-DEB approval for online programmes. The MBA degree is legally equivalent to a campus MBA under UGC 2020 guidelines.

faqs.question: Can I do this MBA while working in a hotel?
faqs.answer: Yes. The online format is designed for working professionals. Hotel industry professionals with shift schedules can access recorded sessions and complete assignments on their own schedule. The 2-year programme accommodates full-time work alongside study.

chooseIf: You work in hotels, tourism, F&B, or travel and want a dedicated Hospitality MBA from a NIRF-ranked university.
You want one of the few NIRF-ranked online Hospitality MBA options at Rs 1,60,000 with zero-cost EMI.
Pune and Maharashtra's tourism ecosystem align with your hospitality career goals.
consider: Amity at Rs 2,25,000 offers Hospitality Management with stronger pan-India brand at a higher fee.
If General Management or Marketing MBA covers your career needs, more options are available across many universities.
If campus-based hospitality training with hands-on labs is a priority, IHMs and other full-time programmes offer that experience.
